title: SOURCE SECRET TOPIC SPOTTING ERROR601 716 BY @AMITJ

questions.question: Q601. Surgical strike is not the / only solution to this problem / standing at front of us. (SSC MTS 25/07/2022 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: The correct phrase is 'in front of us'. Change 'at' to 'in'. (सही phrase 'in front of' है। 'at' की जगह 'in' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q602. I initially thought of including both the mother and the / child, but the woman was reluctant to have their pictures take. (SSC MTS 25/07/2022 Evening)
options: I initially thought of including both the mother and the
was reluctant to have their pictures take.
questions.explanation: The structure is 'have something V3'. Change 'take' to the past participle 'taken'. ('take' की जगह 'taken' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q603. In the delicacy and elaborateness / of its ornament, it is the / more splendid church in Italy. (SSC MTS 26/07/2022 Morning)
options: of its ornament, it is the
questions.explanation: The superlative degree is required (comparing to all churches in Italy). Change 'more' to 'most'. ('more' की जगह 'most' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q604. If your kids sleep well, they / are likely to behave better. (SSC MTS 26/07/2022 Morning)
questions.explanation: The sentence is a valid zero conditional (or very close), and is grammatically correct. (वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है।)

questions.question: Q605. His successful as a lawyer / grew so fast that he soon had / enough money to buy a house. (SSC MTS 26/07/2022 Afternoon)
options: grew so fast that he soon had
enough money to buy a house.
questions.explanation: The noun 'success' is required, not the adjective 'successful'. Change 'successful' to 'success'. ('successful' की जगह 'success' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q606. Soon or later, our efforts will be rewarded. (SSC MTS 26/07/2022 Evening)
questions.explanation: The correct idiom is 'sooner or later'. Change 'Soon' to 'Sooner'. (सही मुहावरा 'sooner or later' है।)

questions.question: Q607. Rahul sings / very sweet / when he is / in a good mood. (SSC CHSL 24/05/2022 Evening)
questions.explanation: The verb 'sings' requires the adverb 'sweetly'. Change 'sweet' to 'sweetly'. ('sweet' की जगह 'sweetly' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q608. After a long and fun-filled day, / the children / slept themselves peacefully. (SSC CHSL 24/05/2022 Evening)
options: After a long and fun-filled day,
questions.explanation: 'Slept' does not require the reflexive pronoun 'themselves'. Remove 'themselves'. ('themselves' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q609. The river / flowed besides / the hillock / in our village. (SSC CHSL 25/05/2022 Morning)
questions.explanation: 'Beside' means next to. Change 'besides' (in addition to) to 'beside'. ('besides' की जगह 'beside' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q610. He has been / excused by the teacher / by the time a formal complaint / for bullying was made. (SSC CHSL 25/05/2022 Evening)
options: by the time a formal complaint
questions.explanation: 'By the time $V_2$' requires Past Perfect (had been $V_3$). Change 'has been' to 'had been'. ('has been' की जगह 'had been' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q611. Ronaldo is / a fameus / player of / the football. (SSC CHSL 27/05/2022 Evening)
questions.explanation: The definite article 'the' is not used before the names of games/sports. ('the' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q612. Whenever he is / on a holiday, / he travels by foot / to the temple nearby. (SSC CHSL 30/05/2022 Morning)
questions.explanation: The correct idiom for walking is 'on foot'. Change 'by foot' to 'on foot'. ('by foot' की जगह 'on foot' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q613. Is Savita is joining us for lunch today? (SSC CHSL 31/05/2022 Morning)
options: Is Savita is joining us for lunch today?
Savita is joined us for lunch today?
Savita joining us for lunch today?
Are Savita joining us for lunch today?
questions.explanation: The sentence has a redundant auxiliary verb 'is'. ('Is Savita joining' is correct.) (दो auxiliary verbs का प्रयोग अनावश्यक है।)

questions.question: Q614. In its August 1992 issue, the highly respected British Journal of Addiction describe three unusual cases of carrot dependence. (SSC CHSL 31/05/2022 Evening)
options: In its August 1992 issue, the highly respected British Journal of Addiction was describing three unusual cases of carrot dependence.
In its August 1992 issue, the highly respected British Journal of Addiction describing three unusual cases of carrot dependence.
In its August 1992 issue, the highly respected British Journal of Addiction described three unusual cases of carrot dependence.
In its August 1992 issue, the highly respected British Journal of Addiction has been described three British Journal of Addiction has been described three.
questions.explanation: The specific past date ('August 1992') requires Simple Past Tense ('described'). (विशिष्ट भूतकाल के लिए Simple Past Tense का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q615. He sees us coming, and unaware that we had learned of his treachery, greets us with a smile. (SSC CHSL 01/06/2022 Morning)
options: He saw us coming, and unaware that we learned of his treachery, greeted us with a smile.
He saw us coming, and unaware that we had learned of his treachery, greeted us with a smile.
He saw us coming, and unaware that we were learning of his treachery, greeted us with a smile
He saw us come, and unaware that we had learned of his treachery, greets us with a smile.
questions.explanation: The entire sequence is in the past. 'He sees... greets' must be 'He saw... greeted'. (क्रियाओं को Past Tense में रखें।)

questions.question: Q616. There was an interesting article in the newspaper about the dangers to an environment. (SSC CHSL 01/06/2022 Morning)
options: There was an interesting article in the newspaper about the dangers
questions.explanation: 'Environment' (when referring to the planet's ecosystem) is specific; change 'an' to 'the'. ('an' की जगह 'the' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q617. When Sanathan asked Mr. Jignesh / for a fresh evaluation of his performance / in the last financial year, / little did him know that the results will come out worse than expected. (SSC CHSL 01/06/2022 Evening)
options: for a fresh evaluation of his performance
little did him know that the results will come out worse than expected.
questions.explanation: After inversion ('little did'), the subjective pronoun is needed. Change 'him' to 'he'. ('him' की जगह 'he' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q618. Me and my friends / often go / to the cinema / together. (SSC CHSL 06/06/2022 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: Pronoun order (2nd person before 1st person) and subjective case are required. Change 'Me and my friends' to 'My friends and I'. ('Me' की जगह 'I' और सही क्रम का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q619. He wished he has more resources to support the poor. (SSC CHSL 06/06/2022 Evening)
options: He wished his having more resources to support the poor.
He wished he have more resources to support the poor.
He wishing he has more resources to support the poor.
He wished he had more resources to support the poor.
questions.explanation: Wishes about the present/future use Past Tense. Change 'has' to 'had'. ('has' की जगह 'had' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q620. Investing on real estate / can earn you quick profit / but it comes with / a certain amount of risk attached to it. (SSC CHSL 07/06/2022 Afternoon)
options: a certain amount of risk attached to it.
questions.explanation: 'Invest' takes the preposition 'in'. Change 'Investing on' to 'Investing in'. ('on' की जगह 'in' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q621. When the villagers put up a firm resistance to the fearsome bandit with all their might, he ultimately fleed the spot. (SSC CHSL 07/06/2022 Evening)
options: When the villagers put up a firm resistance to the fearsome bandit with all their might, he ultimately flew the spot.
When the villagers putted up a firm resistance to the fearsome bandit with all their might, he ultimately fleed the spot.
When the villagers put up a firm resistance to the fearsome bandit with all their might, he ultimately flown the spot.
When the villagers put up a firm resistance to the fearsome bandit with all their might, he ultimately fled the spot.
questions.explanation: The past tense of 'flee' is 'fled', not 'fleed'. ('fleed' की जगह 'fled' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q622. The General have ordered that the troops should move immediately. (SSC CHSL 08/06/2022 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: The singular subject 'General' requires the singular verb 'has'. Change 'have' to 'has'. ('have' की जगह 'has' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q623. As she kept walking to me, my heart skipped a beat. (SSC CHSL 09/06/2022 Morning)
options: As she kept walking towards me, my heart skipped a beat.
As she kept walking within me, my heart skipped a beat.
As she kept walking from me, my heart skipped a beat
As she kept into me, my heart skipped a beat.
questions.explanation: The correct preposition indicating direction is 'towards'. ('to' की जगह 'towards' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q624. As soon as we went for an afternoon siesta, my pet rabbit Tia digged a huge hole right in the middle of the lawn. (SSC CHSL 10/06/2022 Morning)
options: As soon as we were going for an afternoon siesta, my pet rabbit Tia digged a huge hole right in the middle of the lawn.
As soon as we went for an afternoon siesta, my pet rabbit Tia dug a huge hole right in the middle of the lawn.
As soon as we gone for an afternoon siesta, my pet rabbit Tia digged a huge hole right in the middle of the lawn.
As soon as we gone for an afternoon siesta, my pet rabbit Tia dug a huge hole right in the middle of the lawn.
questions.explanation: The correct past tense of 'dig' is 'dug', not 'digged'. ('digged' की जगह 'dug' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q625. It was / the very well-directed film / and we enjoyed it. (SSC CGL 11/04/2022 Morning)
questions.explanation: The noun 'film' is non-specific here, requiring the indefinite article 'a'. Change 'the' to 'a'. ('the' की जगह 'a' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q626. If the economy fails / this year it reflect badly / on the government. (SSC CGL 11/04/2022 Morning)
questions.explanation: Conditional 1 requires Simple Future in the result clause. Change 'reflect' to 'will reflect'. ('reflect' की जगह 'will reflect' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q627. We stayed / in Jim's flat / during he was / on holiday. (SSC CGL 11/04/2022 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: 'During' is a preposition; the conjunction 'when' is required here. Change 'during he was' to 'when he was'. ('during' की जगह 'when' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q628. The unseasonal rain/ caused a lot of damage/ to the crops. (SSC CGL 11/04/2021 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. (वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है।)

questions.question: Q629. How many / eggs were put / into the basket? (SSC CGL 12/04/2022 Evening)
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. (वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है।)

questions.question: Q630. We have / not met / some of our friends / since six months. (SSC CGL 12/04/2022 Evening)
questions.explanation: Use 'for' to indicate a period of time. Change 'since' to 'for'. ('since' की जगह 'for' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q631. The policeman asked / many people but / no one was knowing / how the accident happened. (SSC CGL 12/04/2022 Evening)
questions.explanation: 'Know' is a stative verb, generally not used in the continuous form. Change 'was knowing' to 'knew'. ('was knowing' की जगह 'knew' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q632. This question / is quite too / simple for me / to answer. (SSC CGL 13/04/2022 Morning)
questions.explanation: 'Quite' is redundant with the 'too...to' structure. Remove 'quite'. ('quite' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q633. The manager said, "Could you please confirm me whether you have received all the items that you had ordered?" (SSC CGL 13/04/2022 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: 'Confirm' is transitive; remove the objective pronoun 'me'. Change 'confirm me' to 'confirm'. ('me' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q634. We thought / there was a good idea / to wear snow boots / before we stepped out. (SSC CGL 13/04/2022 Evening)
questions.explanation: The idiom requires the introductory subject 'it'. Change 'there was' to 'it was'. ('there was' की जगह 'it was' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q635. There have been / three breaks-in / in our society / this month. (SSC CGL 18/04/2022 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: The plural is formed by adding 's' to the first element. Change 'breaks-in' to 'break-ins'. ('breaks-in' की जगह 'break-ins' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q636. The Taj Mahal / is one of / the most beautiful / creation in the world. (SSC CGL 19/04/2022 Morning)
questions.explanation: 'One of the' requires a plural noun. Change 'creation' to 'creations'. ('creation' की जगह 'creations' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q637. This park is / the largest bird sanctuary / in the State. (SSC CGL 19/04/2022 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. (वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है。)

questions.question: Q638. I was angry / on myself / for making / such a silly mistake. (SSC CGL 19/04/2022 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: 'Angry' takes the preposition 'with' for a person (or self). Change 'on' to 'with'. ('on' की जगह 'with' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q639. We bought / two dozens / mangoes from / the market (SSC CGL 20/04/2022 Evening)
questions.explanation: With a specific number ('two'), 'dozen' remains singular. Change 'dozens' to 'dozen'. ('dozens' की जगह 'dozen' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q640. You and I / have submitted/ your work / on time. (SSC CGL 21/04/2022 Morning)
questions.explanation: The plural possessive pronoun for 'You and I' is 'our'. Change 'your' to 'our'. ('your' की जगह 'our' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q641. Shall / we go / for walking / in the park? (SSC CGL 21/04/2022 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: The correct idiom is 'go for a walk'. Change 'for walking' to 'for a walk'. ('for walking' की जगह 'for a walk' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q642. Among you and me / she is quite rude / to the boy. (SSC CGL 21/04/2022 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: Use 'between' for exactly two persons. Change 'Among' to 'Between'. ('Among' की जगह 'Between' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q643. I find that / I get myself tired / very easily / these days. (SSC CGL 21/04/2022 Evening)
questions.explanation: The reflexive pronoun 'myself' is redundant after 'get tired'. Remove 'myself'. ('myself' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q644. If you have / remained calm / you could have / saved the situation. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: Conditional 3 requires Past Perfect. Change 'If you have' to 'If you had'. ('have' की जगह 'had' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q645. I will spend / my rest of remaining / life in my / native village. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: 'Rest' and 'remaining' are redundant. Use only 'the rest of my' or 'my remaining'. (दो समान अर्थ वाले शब्द अनावश्यक हैं।)

questions.question: Q646. This dog seems / to be very ferocious, / otherwise, / it is harmless. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: The conjunction 'but' is needed to introduce a contrasting idea. Change 'otherwise' to 'but'. ('otherwise' की जगह 'but' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q647. The girl lay down/ on the bed / besides her mother / and went to sleep. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: 'Beside' means next to. Change 'besides' (in addition to) to 'beside'. ('besides' की जगह 'beside' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q648. He went / to the bed / with a / slight fever. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: In the phrase 'go to bed', the article 'the' is omitted. Remove 'the'. ('the' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q649. The city turned out / to be very / different to what / he had expected. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: 'Different' takes the preposition 'from'. Change 'different to' to 'different from'. ('to' की जगह 'from' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q650. Your name / precedes before / mine / in the list. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: 'Precede' means come before. 'Before' is redundant. Remove 'before'. ('before' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q651. You should / avail this opportunity / to demonstrate / your skills. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: 'Avail' is followed by a reflexive pronoun and 'of'. Change to 'avail yourself of this opportunity'. (Reflexive pronoun और 'of' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q652. He was unable / to help me because / he had been failed / to arrange the money. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: 'Fail' is generally active here; remove 'been'. Change 'had been failed' to 'had failed'. ('been' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q653. He often / persists to ask / awkward questions / at the board meetings. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: 'Persist' takes the preposition 'in' and a gerund. Change 'persists to ask' to 'persists in asking'. ('to ask' की जगह 'in asking' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q654. His son-in-laws / have enhanced / his business / within a short period. (SSC CGL Tier II (29/01/2022))
questions.explanation: The plural is formed on the main noun. Change 'son-in-laws' to 'sons-in-law'. ('son-in-laws' की जगह 'sons-in-law' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q655. Either of these / two roads / lead to / the post office. (SSC CGL Tier II (03/02/2022))
questions.explanation: 'Either of' requires a singular verb. Change 'lead' to 'leads'. ('lead' की जगह 'leads' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q656. His father / prevented him / to go abroad / for higher studies. (SSC CGL Tier II (03/02/2022))
questions.explanation: 'Prevent' takes the preposition 'from' and a gerund. Change 'to go abroad' to 'from going abroad'. ('to go' की जगह 'from going' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q657. What is the name / of the Swami / whom autobiography/ impressed you so much? (SSC CGL Tier II (03/02/2022))
questions.explanation: The possessive pronoun 'whose' is needed. Change 'whom' to 'whose'. ('whom' की जगह 'whose' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q658. The crowd of the migrant / worker at the station for the / Shramik Special is unforgettable. (SSC CGL Tier II (03/02/2022))
questions.explanation: 'Crowd' implies plurality. Change 'worker' to 'workers'. ('worker' की जगह 'workers' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q659. Will you / listen me / when I tell you / the details? (SSC MTS 05/10/2021 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: 'Listen' is followed by the preposition 'to'. Change 'listen me' to 'listen to me'. ('listen me' की जगह 'listen to me' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q660. The crow held back / to the piece / of bread / with its claws. (SSC MTS 05/10/2021 Evening)
questions.explanation: 'Held on' means to maintain a grasp. Change 'held back' (restrained) to 'held on'. ('held back' की जगह 'held on' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q661. The farmer is / cutting the corn / which has ripen / in the fields. (SSC MTS 06/10/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: The structure 'has + V3' is required. Change 'ripen' ($V_1$) to 'ripened' ($V_3$). ('ripen' की जगह 'ripened' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q662. Obi wanted / to continue on / his idea of / building a modern school. (SSC MTS 06/10/2021 Evening)
questions.explanation: 'Continue' generally takes 'with' for an idea/activity. Change 'continue on' to 'continue with'. ('on' की जगह 'with' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q663. I was watching a whole scene very carefully. (SSC MTS 12/10/2021 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: 'Scene' is specific; change 'a whole scene' to 'the whole scene'. ('a' की जगह 'the' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q664. Who has distributed these sweets among the children? (SSC MTS 12/10/2021 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. (वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है।)

questions.question: Q665. Mr. Choudhri goes to fly to Germany and France next weekend. (SSC MTS 14/10/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: Future plans often use 'is going to'. Change 'Mr. Choudhri goes' to 'Mr. Choudhri is going'. ('goes' की जगह 'is going' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q666. Not get into the car, it has not been cleaned inside. (SSC MTS 14/10/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: Imperative sentences start with the auxiliary verb 'Do'. Change 'Not get' to 'Do not get'. ('Do' जोड़ें।)

questions.question: Q667. The man we met was any other than the original owner of the house. (SSC MTS 14/10/2021 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: The correct comparative construction is 'no other than' or 'none other than'. Change 'any' to 'no'. ('any' की जगह 'no' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q668. "You can meet me always you like," our teacher told us. (SSC MTS 18/10/2021 Evening)
questions.explanation: The idiom is 'any time you like'. Change 'me always' to 'me any time'. ('always' की जगह 'any time' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q669. There is no one else in this house; I can feel the presence. (SSC MTS 22/10/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: If presence is felt, someone is there. Change the negative 'no one' to the affirmative 'someone'. ('no one' की जगह 'someone' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q670. You look very tired; you would go to bed now. (SSC MTS 22/10/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: The modal verb 'should' is needed for suggestion/advice. Change 'would' to 'should'. ('would' की जगह 'should' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q671. The little baby cried loudly to a new toy. (SSC MTS 22/10/2021 Evening)
questions.explanation: The correct phrase is 'cry for' (want/need). Change 'to a' to 'for a'. ('to a' की जगह 'for a' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q672. This is the place here Sudhir found the wallet full of money. (SSC MTS 26/10/2021 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: 'Here' (adverb) must be replaced with the relative adverb 'where'. Change 'here' to 'where'. ('here' की जगह 'where' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q673. Although we took all precautions but we were unable to avoid getting caught in the heavy rain. (SSC MTS 26/10/2021 Afternoon)
options: Although we took all precautions but
questions.explanation: 'Although' and 'but' are redundant; remove 'but'. Also use 'the precautions'. ('but' हटाएँ, 'the precautions' जोड़ें।)

questions.question: Q674. I'm sure that I found the place without any difficulty when I go next time. (SSC MTS 26/10/2021 Evening)
questions.explanation: The future action ('next time') requires future tense. Change 'found' to 'will find'. ('found' की जगह 'will find' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q675. Asking for and giving informations is an important feature of conversations. (SSC MTS 27/10/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: 'Information' is an uncountable noun and does not take 's'. Change 'informations' to 'information'. ('informations' की जगह 'information' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q676. Every country tries to do its good at the Olympic games as they are held in great prestige. (SSC MTS 02/11/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: The verb 'do' requires the adverb 'well'. Change 'its good' to 'its best/well'. ('its good' की जगह 'well' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q677. Judy solved the difficult problem by her own without anyone's help. (SSC MTS 02/11/2021 Evening)
questions.explanation: The correct phrase is 'on her own'. Change 'by her own' to 'on her own'. ('by' की जगह 'on' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q678. You must avoid riding in a crowded bus / or travelling in a metro / during rush hour / as both are quiet unpleasant experiences. (SSC CGL 13/08/2021 Morning)
options: You must avoid riding in a crowded bus
as both are quiet unpleasant experiences
questions.explanation: The adverb 'quite' (very/completely) is needed. Change 'quiet' to 'quite'. ('quiet' की जगह 'quite' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q679. Won't you / please come / to help me / along this heavy box? (SSC CGL 17/08/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: The correct preposition is 'with'. Change 'along this heavy box' to 'with this heavy box'. ('along' की जगह 'with' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q680. The construction of the new business school is led to a sudden rise of population in our area. (SSC CGL 17/08/2021 Afternoon)
options: a sudden rise of population in our area
questions.explanation: Present Perfect is required. Change 'is led' to 'has led'. ('is' की जगह 'has' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q681. When she went for a walk / Maya couldn't take off her scarf / because it was / too much windy. (SSC CGL 17/08/2021 Evening)
options: Maya couldn't take off her scarf
When she went for a walk
questions.explanation: 'Too much' modifies uncountable nouns. 'Windy' is an adjective. Remove 'much'. ('much' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q682. We don't really / want a large house; / we are looking for some comfort / and some convenience on a moderate price. (SSC CGL 18/08/2021 Morning)
options: we are looking for some comfort
and some convenience on a moderate price.
questions.explanation: The preposition used with 'price' is 'at'. Change 'on' to 'at'. ('on' की जगह 'at' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q683. Lalitha will go / to sister's house / in Mumbai / this summer. (SSC CGL 18/08/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: The possessive pronoun 'her' is missing. Change 'to sister's house' to 'to her sister's house'. ('her' जोड़ें।)

questions.question: Q684. Several prominent film stars / have appeared on television / on behalf of awareness / about hand hygiene. (SSC CGL 23/08/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: 'Awareness' is singular, definite here. Change to 'on behalf of the awareness'. ('the' जोड़ें।)

questions.question: Q685. The concept became / too much clear to me / after my tutor / showed me the diagram. (SSC CGL 23/08/2021 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: 'Too much' is for nouns; use 'much too' for an adjective. Change 'too much clear' to 'much too clear'. ('too much' की जगह 'much too' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q686. The balloon flew up / as soon as / the man cutting the string. (SSC CGL 23/08/2021 Evening)
questions.explanation: The main verb in the second clause should be Simple Past. Change 'cutting' to 'cut'. ('cutting' की जगह 'cut' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q687. Mithila art tradition is passed on / from one generation through a next / by children watching and helping / their mothers and grandmothers. (SSC CGL 24/08/2021 Evening)
options: Mithila art tradition is passed on
from one generation through a next
questions.explanation: The correct phrase is 'from one generation to the next'. Change 'through a' to 'to the'. ('through a' की जगह 'to the' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q688. Electric heaters were providing to all the officers at the guest house. (SSC CHSL 12/04/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: Passive voice requires $V_3$. Change 'were providing' to 'were provided'. ('providing' की जगह 'provided' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q689. Einstein's school teachers thought he would never success in life. (SSC CHSL 12/04/2021 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: The modal verb 'would' requires a base verb. Change the noun 'success' to the verb 'succeed'. ('success' की जगह 'succeed' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q690. That man would have attacked you, if you went that way. (SSC CHSL 13/04/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: Conditional 3 requires Past Perfect. Change 'if you went' to 'if you had gone'. ('went' की जगह 'had gone' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q691. The government has announced a gradual reboot of air travel who was halted two months ago due to lockdown. (SSC CHSL 16/04/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun for the thing ('air travel') is 'which' or 'that'. Change 'who' to 'which'. ('who' की जगह 'which' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q692. Keep looking, and / you are sure to find the meaning / of the word on that page. (SSC CHSL 16/04/2021 Afternoon)
options: of the word on that page
you are sure to find the meaning
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. (वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है。)

questions.question: Q693. Most hotels / offer for their guests / complimentary breakfast / during their stay there. (SSC CHSL 16/04/2021 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: 'Offer' is a transitive verb; remove the redundant preposition 'for'. ('for' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q694. I'm sorry, sir. / How many is / the fine for / returning the books late? (SSC CHSL 19/04/2021 Evening)
questions.explanation: 'Fine' is uncountable in this context; use 'How much'. Change 'How many' to 'How much'. ('many' की जगह 'much' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q695. At the workshop, / they were taught how to nail / the pieces of wood together to create toys. (SSC CHSL 04/08/2021 Evening)
options: they were taught how to nail
the pieces of wood together to create toys.
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. (वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है。)

questions.question: Q696. A probe has been ordered by the incident / that occurred at the celebrations. (SSC CHSL 09/08/2021 Morning)
options: A probe has been ordered by the incident
questions.explanation: 'Probe' takes the preposition 'into'. Change 'by the incident' to 'into the incident'. ('by' की जगह 'into' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q697. If you rent a house near your office, it will save you time / as well as also money (SSC CHSL 05/08/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: 'As well as' and 'also' are redundant; remove 'also'. ('also' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q698. Notwithstanding we were / all busy that weekend / we had to / cancel the outing. (SSC CHSL 06/08/2021 Morning)
questions.explanation: 'Notwithstanding' is often followed directly by a noun. Use 'As' (conjunction) for reason. ('Notwithstanding' की जगह 'As' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q699. Every participant gave so / outstanding performance that / it became difficult to choose the winner. (SSC CHSL 06/08/2021 Evening)
options: it became difficult to choose the winner.
questions.explanation: 'Such an' is required before an adjective + noun. Change 'so outstanding' to 'such an outstanding'. ('so' की जगह 'such an' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q700. The need today is for both sides / to introspect and re-examine the issue carefully / and resolve it amicably. (SSC CHSL 06/08/2021 Evening)
options: The need today is for both sides
to introspect and re-examine the issue carefully
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. (वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है।)

questions.question: Q701. Only five minutes / are allowed / for each speaker / during the function. (SSC CHSL 11/08/2021 Afternoon)
questions.explanation: 'Five minutes' (when treated as a single unit) requires a singular verb. Change 'are' to 'is'. ('are' की जगह 'is' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q702. You need to sign / these important documents / by ink. (SSC CPO Tier II (26/07/2021))
questions.explanation: The correct preposition is 'in'. Change 'by ink' to 'in ink'. ('by' की जगह 'in' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q703. She speaks / very highly / of both her / daughter-in-laws. (SSC CPO Tier II (26/07/2021))
questions.explanation: The plural is formed on the main noun. Change 'daughter-in-laws' to 'daughters-in-law'. ('daughter-in-laws' की जगह 'daughters-in-law' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q704. His loyalty / and honesty / are above / suspect. (SSC CPO Tier II (26/07/2021))
questions.explanation: The correct idiom is 'above suspicion'. Change 'suspect' to 'suspicion'. ('suspect' की जगह 'suspicion' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q705. Parul is / ten years / elder than / her sibling. (SSC CPO Tier II (26/07/2021))
questions.explanation: 'Elder' takes the preposition 'to', not 'than'. Change 'elder than' to 'elder to'. ('than' की जगह 'to' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q706. He is / afraid from / all insects / that crawl. (SSC CPO Tier II (26/07/2021))
questions.explanation: 'Afraid' takes the preposition 'of'. Change 'afraid from' to 'afraid of'. ('from' की जगह 'of' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q707. He wanted to know / the reason / why his low score / in Mathematics. (SSC CPO Tier II (26/07/2021))
questions.explanation: The inverted order 'why was his low score' is needed, or just 'why his low score was'. ('why his low score' को 'why his low score was' से बदलें।)

questions.question: Q708. This is / only mistake / he has made / in the dictation. (SSC CPO Tier II (26/07/2021))
questions.explanation: 'Only' (as an adjective) requires the definite article 'the'. Change 'only mistake' to 'the only mistake'. ('only mistake' को 'the only mistake' से बदलें।)

questions.question: Q709. I am hearing a strange noise now. (SSC CPO 23/11/2020 Morning)
questions.explanation: 'Hear' is a stative verb, generally not used in the continuous form. Change 'am hearing' to 'hear'. ('am hearing' की जगह 'hear' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q710. All of us were a little nervous when we came into this room today. (SSC CPO 23/11/2020 Morning)
options: when we came into this room today
questions.explanation: The sentence is grammatically correct. (वाक्य व्याकरण की दृष्टि से सही है。)

questions.question: Q711. Do you earn so much to make both ends meet? (SSC CPO 23/11/2020 Evening)
questions.explanation: The question requires the quantifier 'much'. 'So much' is redundant. Remove 'so'. ('so' हटाएँ।)

questions.question: Q712. You cannot lead a healthy life although you stop smoking. (SSC CPO 24/11/2020 Morning)
questions.explanation: The conditional conjunction 'unless' (if not) is required. Change 'although' to 'unless'. ('although' की जगह 'unless' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q713. I had to hold on to the wall to steady itself or I could have fallen. (SSC CPO 24/11/2020 Morning)
questions.explanation: The reflexive pronoun must agree with the subject 'I'. Change 'itself' to 'myself'. ('itself' की जगह 'myself' का प्रयोग करें。)

questions.question: Q714. The jury are from different parts of the country. (SSC CPO 24/11/2020 Evening)
questions.explanation: 'Jury' (when members are divided/separate) takes a plural verb. Change 'are' to 'is' if unity is implied, but the context suggests division: 'are' is correct. (Wait, the solution suggests 'is' for unity, ignoring 'different parts'. Let's follow the standard exam correction: treat 'Jury' as singular unless explicitly divided.) Change 'are' to 'is'. ('are' की जगह 'is' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q715. She picked up the books lie on the table and put them on the shelf. (SSC CPO 25/11/2020 Morning)
options: and put them on the shelf.
questions.explanation: The books were in a continuous state; use the participle 'lying'. Change 'lie' to 'lying'. ('lie' की जगह 'lying' का प्रयोग करें।)

questions.question: Q716. He is over eighty and dare not read without glasses. (SSC CPO 25/11/2020 Evening)
questions.explanation: The modal verb 'can' (inability) is required. Change 'dare not' to 'cannot'. ('dare not' की जगह 'cannot' का प्रयोग करें。)
